primary_cdc_lyme.title: CDC Lyme Disease Surveillance Data
primary_cdc_lyme.description: National yearly, state, and CSV downloads for cases 1996-2023

primary_cdc_lyme.title: CDC Lyme Disease Case Maps
primary_cdc_lyme.description: County-level dot maps for 1995, 2010, 2023 and 2023 county CSV with historical columns

primary_cdc_lyme.title: CDC MMWR: Surveillance for Lyme Disease After Implementation of a Revised Case Definition — United States, 2022
primary_cdc_lyme.description: 2022 case counts, incidence changes, high-incidence jurisdictions

primary_cdc_lyme.title: CDC Lyme Disease Facts and Statistics / Index
primary_cdc_lyme.description: Overview, 89,000+ cases in 2023, links to data

cdc_alpha_gal.title: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Syndrome Cases — United States, 2017–2022
cdc_alpha_gal.description: 90,018 positive tests 2017-2022, yearly breakdown, geographic (county-level rates), demographics, methodology (Eurofins Viracor lab data)

cdc_alpha_gal.title: CDC About Alpha-gal Syndrome
cdc_alpha_gal.description: >110,000 suspected cases 2010-2022, estimate up to 450,000 affected

massachusetts_dph.title: Massachusetts Tick-borne Disease Surveillance Summaries and Data
massachusetts_dph.description: 10-year morbidity report, yearly Lyme cases 2015-2026 YTD, surveillance changes noted, annual reports 2019-2025

nantucket_and_local.title: Reports of Lyme disease in Nantucket County, Massachusetts (tickcheck.com / CDC data)
nantucket_and_local.description: Yearly confirmed cases 2000-2020 (723 total), sourced from CDC surveillance

nantucket_and_local.title: Nantucket Cottage Hospital / Town of Nantucket Ticks Page
nantucket_and_local.description: Local context, links to infectious disease reports

nantucket_and_local.title: Phillips et al. (2001) - Lyme disease and preventive behaviors in residents of Nantucket Island

johns_hopkins.title: Johns Hopkins Lyme and Tickborne Diseases Dashboard / Tracker
johns_hopkins.description: Interactive maps, data explorer, county-level visualizations, references to CDC data

johns_hopkins.title: Johns Hopkins Lyme Disease Page
johns_hopkins.description: ~500,000 diagnosed/treated per year estimates, geographic expansion info

literature_and_other.title: Mead et al. (2024) - Lyme Disease Surveillance and Epidemiology in the United States
Kugeler et al. (2021) - Estimating the Frequency of Lyme Disease Diagnoses, United States, 2010-2018
ArcGIS Hub - Reported Cases of Lyme Disease by County of Residence Map, United States (2000-2020)
Various MMWR and Statista references for incidence rates and historical summaries
literature_and_other.url: Multiple (e.g., https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/, Statista Lyme incidence by state)

web_search_and_browse_sources: web:31 to web:74 from initial and follow-up searches (CDC pages, MMWR, mass.gov, PubMed, JHU, etc.)
Specific browsed URLs: surveillance-data-1.html, lyme-disease-case-map.html, mm7230a2.htm, mass.gov tickborne lists, tickcheck.com Nantucket page

historical_tick_distribution.title: Spatial Distribution of Counties in the Continental United States With Records of Occurrence of Amblyomma americanum (Ixodida: Ixodidae)
historical_tick_distribution.authors: Yuri P. Springer, Lars Eisen, Lorenza Beati, Angela M. James, Rebecca J. Eisen
historical_tick_distribution.description: Compilation of 18,121 collection records (1898–2012) with county-level classifications as 'established' or 'reported'. Includes decadal and cumulative maps. Best publicly available historical county-level data for Lone Star tick distribution. Used for future time-dynamic versions of the purple Verified Tick Population layer.

expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.title: County-Level Distribution of Ixodes scapularis and Ixodes pacificus — United States, 2015
expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.description: National county-level maps of established and reported blacklegged tick populations. Strong candidate for expanding the Ixodes portion of the purple density layer.

expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.title: CDC Tick Surveillance Data and Maps (2025)
expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.description: Latest multi-species county-level presence data (including Ixodes and Amblyomma americanum). Excellent source for updating 2025 purple tick layers.

expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.title: Lyme Disease Cases by County of Residence — United States, 2023
expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.description: County-level Lyme case data with downloadable CSV. Useful for extending the red layer time series and county-level detail.

expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.title: Climate Change Indicators: Lyme Disease (EPA)
expanded_surveillance_2015_2025.description: Long-term national and regional Lyme incidence trends tied to climate variables. Good supporting context.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: County-Level Distribution of Ixodes scapularis and Ixodes pacificus (Blacklegged and Western Blacklegged Ticks) — United States, 2015
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Updated national county-level maps for blacklegged ticks. Key for future Ixodes-focused purple layer expansion.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: CDC Tick Surveillance Data and Maps
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Latest 2025 county-level data for multiple tick species including Lone Star and blacklegged. Primary source for current purple blob updates.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Lyme Disease Cases by County of Residence — United States, 2023
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: County-level Lyme data. Used to expand red dots to many more states in testnet.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Estimating the Frequency of Lyme Disease Diagnoses, United States, 2010–2018
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: ~476,000 annual diagnoses estimate. Important context for underreporting disclaimers.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Climate Change Indicators: Lyme Disease
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Long-term Lyme trends linked to climate. Useful for explanatory text.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Johns Hopkins Lyme and Tickborne Diseases Dashboard
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.authors: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Interactive county-level visualizations for validation and future features.

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Syndrome Cases — United States, 2017–2022
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Core Alpha-gal data source (already in use).

new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.title: Spatial Distribution of Counties With Records of Amblyomma americanum (Springer et al. 2014 references)
new_recommended_sources_for_expansion.description: Foundational historical Lone Star tick data for the purple layer.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: 2023 Lyme Disease Cases by County (CDC CSV)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Exact county-level reported Lyme case counts for 2023 across all US counties. Ideal for future county heatmap / choropleth layer.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Lyme Disease Cases by State or Locality 2008–2023 (CDC CSV)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: State-level Lyme counts and incidence rates over time. Excellent for trends, validation, and fallback data.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Reported Tickborne Disease Cases by County 2019–2022 (All TBD Excel)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level cases for Lyme, Babesiosis, Anaplasmosis, Ehrlichiosis, Spotted Fever Rickettsiosis and more. Broadest county tickborne dataset.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Syndrome Cases (MMWR 2023)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level suspected AGS cases from lab data (2017–2022). Highest per-capita counties detailed. Cross-reference with census for rates.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Public Use Ixodes County Table 2026 (Blacklegged Tick Surveillance)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level established/reported status for Ixodes scapularis + associated pathogens (up to 2025 data). Best current data for actual tick presence layers.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Lone Star Tick Surveillance (Amblyomma americanum)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level distribution data for the primary Alpha-gal vector. Downloadable datasets from the CDC tick surveillance hub.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: CDC Tick Data Research Hub (All Species)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Central index to all current tick species surveillance maps, data files, and species-specific Excels (Ixodes, Lone Star, etc.).

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: CDC WONDER NNDSS Annual Summary (Notifiable Diseases)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Interactive query system for national notifiable disease data including Lyme. Useful for custom year/state extracts and incidence.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: 2023 Lyme Disease Cases by County (exact counts)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level reported Lyme cases for 2023 — direct drop-in for heatmap. Best single-year county file available.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Lyme Cases by State 2008–2023 (historical trends)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: State-level counts and incidence over 16 years. Great for animation fill-in years and trend validation.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: All Tickborne Diseases by County 2019–2022
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Lyme + Anaplasmosis + Babesiosis + Ehrlichiosis + Spotted Fever + more — county level. Broadest county dataset CDC publishes.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Tickborne Diseases by County 2016–2019 (older baseline)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Historic county-level for multiple diseases. Fills earlier animation years.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: 2025 Lone Star Tick Surveillance (Alpha-gal vector)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level established/reported status for Lone Star ticks — updated through 2025. MISSOURI GOLD — established in nearly every county.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Ixodes (Blacklegged) Tick Surveillance 2025–2026
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level established/reported for Lyme vector. Best for Lyme density layers and multi-species purple expansion.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Cases (MMWR 2023)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level suspected AGS from 357k+ lab tests (2017–2022). Missouri, OK, AR, KY, VA are hotspots — raw county tables inside.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Missouri Alpha-gal Syndrome Official Estimate
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 7,300–34,400 Missourians affected since 2021 (DHSS). Direct counter to misinformation about tick presence.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Missouri Tickborne Disease Story Map
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Interactive DHSS dashboard — Ehrlichiosis, Lyme, ticks, etc. Visual + case trends for MO-specific layers.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: NEON Tick Abundance & Pathogen Data (ecological)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Standardized drag-sampling across US sites — abundance + pathogens (2014–present). High-quality density data, not just cases.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: NEON Tick Pathogen Status
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Pathogen testing results from NEON ticks (Borrelia, Anaplasma, Babesia, Ehrlichia, etc.). Pairs with abundance for risk modeling.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Arkansas DH Tickborne Disease Interactive Dashboard
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level data for Alpha-gal, RMSF, Ehrlichiosis, Tularemia, etc. by year (2021–2025). Goldmine — select any Arkansas county for exact numbers.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Alpha-gal Syndrome Arkansas Surveillance (2023–July 2025)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 5,167 reported cases across 49/75 Arkansas counties; 403 confirmed. Peer-reviewed study with demographics and northern AR hotspot data.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Arkansas Alpha-gal Fact Sheet & Reporting
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Official Arkansas Department of Health page with fact sheet and data download links.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Arkansas Alpha-gal Mandatory Reporting Data (2023–2025)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Mandatory reporting numbers: 304 cases in 2023, 2,613 in 2024, 3,781 in 2025. Northern Arkansas is the clear hotspot.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: California Lyme Disease Cases 2024 (CDPH Annual Report)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 96 total reported Lyme cases in California for 2024 (county-level breakdowns included).

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Missouri DHSS Tickborne Disease Main Page
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Official Missouri DHSS page covering reportable tickborne diseases (RMSF, Ehrlichiosis, Tularemia, Lyme-like illness) and general surveillance information with links to reference materials.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Show-Me Ticks Citizen Science Study
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 3,811 ticks collected across 33 Missouri counties (2019–2021); 89% were Lone Star ticks. Excellent baseline abundance data from citizen science.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Ixodes Pathogen County Table 2026
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: County-level pathogens found in I. pacificus and I. scapularis (Borrelia, Anaplasma, etc.). Direct infection data for Lyme/anaplasmosis in Western ticks.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: California Lyme & Tick Surveillance Story Map
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Interactive county-level Lyme incidence (2014–2024) + I. pacificus testing results (positives for Borrelia). Best visual for CA — click counties for exact case counts and tick testing data.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Oregon Tickborne Disease Statistics & Maps
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Graphs and maps for Lyme, RMSF, relapsing fever, etc. (low local Lyme). Shows how rare local Lyme is in Oregon.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Washington Tick Submission Dashboard
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Citizen-submitted ticks by species, county, year, host. Excellent passive surveillance data from public submissions.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Washington Alpha-gal Case Report (I. pacificus linked)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 2025 documented AGS case from local Western blacklegged tick bite. Rare West Coast AGS evidence.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: CDC Tick Bite Data Tracker (ER Visits for Tick Bites)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: National syndromic surveillance of emergency department visits for tick bites — by week, month, region, age, sex. Best real-time proxy for disease burden. 2026 spring data showed highest ER visit rates since 2017 in Northeast + Midwest.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Missouri ER Visits for Tick Bites (2026 Spring Surge)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Midwest (incl. Missouri) sharp rise: 39 per 100,000 in March → 137 per 100,000 in April 2026. Directly tied to Lone Star activity and rising AGS warnings. University Health (KC) opened dedicated AGS hotline.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Oklahoma Alpha-gal Syndrome Mandatory Reporting (SB 1644, 2026)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: SB 1644 signed May 2026 — AGS becomes reportable Nov 1, 2026. Seroprevalence ~35% (military recruits); estimated 70,000–140,000 potential full AGS cases. Multiple reports of repeated ER visits for anaphylaxis.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Texas Tickborne Disease Cases (DSHS 2020–2026)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Low Lyme (21 cases in 2024, 19 in 2025 YTD); Ehrlichiosis steady (13–14 cases/year). Lone Star tick widespread across the state — expected high AGS burden despite low reported Lyme.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Texas DSHS Zoonotic Cases 2020–2026 (Detailed)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Lyme: 19–21 cases/year (mostly imported). Ehrlichiosis: 13–14/year. Spotted Fever Rickettsiosis: 15–18/year. Useful for a broader 'tickborne illness' red dot layer beyond just Lyme.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Texas Alpha-gal Syndrome – Growing Incidence 2026
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Lone Star ticks widespread across Texas. AGS cases increasing, but no mandatory reporting yet → no county-level numbers available. Good for Texas-wide purple blob popup notes.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: Alpha-gal IgE Prevalence in U.S. Military Recruits (2024 study)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: 6.0% overall positivity; highest in AR (39%), OK (35%), MO (29%). Closely tracks Lone Star tick range. Strong South burden indicator.

cdc_direct_downloads_2026.title: AGS Healthcare Utilization Studies (Anaphylaxis & ER/Hospital Burden)
cdc_direct_downloads_2026.description: Multiple studies show >50% of AGS patients experience anaphylaxis requiring ED visits (some multiple hospitalizations). Delayed diagnosis drives high costs. Underdiagnosed AGS in South/Midwest leads to repeated ER visits.
